qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Three dev provenance completeness gaps found by Codex in cycle 20260522-080646: (D1) QA only checks over-reporting, not under-reporting; (D2) parallel aggregate dev-report missing baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, observed_preexisting; (D3) files_created derivation misses staged new files
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All three prose-text gaps (D1, D2, D3) are present and correctly implemented in the three modified files. The implementation correctly addresses the user's stated requirement. The FAIL verdict is due to 6 unfilled TEST_INCOMPLETE stubs in tests/generated/20260524-125300-D/ (Phase 5 hard rule), not due to AC content failures.

complaint_element_pointer.selector: N/A — prose markdown changes, no DOM element
complaint_element_pointer.how_located: Static grep against modified files; non-UI change

qa.overall_assessment: All 6 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC6) are satisfied by the prose text changes in agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, and agents/dev.md. The implementation correctly addresses all three provenance gaps (D1 under-reporting check, D2 aggregate field additions, D3 staged-file derivation). However, Phase 5 manifest verification finds 6 active tests with TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels. Per agents/qa.md Phase 5 item 2, these are critical findings with primary_cause: dev_implementation. The test-writer ran before dev (trigger_gate.fired=true), meaning dev had the opportunity to fill in the test bodies but did not. Verdict is FAIL on the TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinel rule. The AC prose content itself passes.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: The full diff of commands/dev.md against baseline SHA f79fba1c also includes a location-complaint tokenization change (replacing mechanical token-set arithmetic with semantic LLM judgment) from commit 9c7fd8c (a parallel session bulk commit predating the D task). This hunk is unrelated to D2 but is within the current file content. The D2-specific changes (lines 706-708, 735-737, 743, 755-756, 766) are cleanly confined to the aggregate construction rule and example JSON. No AC was written for the tokenization change; it is a separate concurrent-task change. Codex flagged this as a BLOCKER/MAJOR, but per QA scope rules, the D task's ACs only cover the D2 aggregate fields — not all changes in the files. The tokenization change was not introduced by this dev cycle and should be attributed to its own task.

The diff of agents/qa.md against baseline also includes a Phase 5 trigger condition change (from 'when test-writer ran upstream' to 'when complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high'). This was co-committed in the same bulk commit as the D1 changes (commit 4991a9d). The change is a related improvement to the Phase 5 trigger alignment. No AC was written for this change specifically, but it is within the D1 fix scope area (agents/qa.md provenance section).

Reverse provenance check (Codex finding 2): git diff --name-only f79fba1c shows 199 files changed. Dev only declared 3 in files_modified. The 196 extra files are all committed changes from other parallel tasks (task IDs: 122910, 122947, 123039, 125300-A, 125300-B, 125300-C, 125300-push, 133650, and test suite additions). The working tree at QA time only has 1 uncommitted modified file (docs/dev/INDEX.md) and none of the 3 target files (they are committed). This is the multi-agent parallel session scenario where the baseline SHA predates all concurrent tasks. A mechanical application of the reverse provenance check rule would generate 196 files_modified_underreport_violation findings for changes by other agents. This is a known limitation of the per-task git-diff provenance check in parallel sessions and is out_of_scope for this task's verdict. The rule is designed for single-dev scenarios where git diff captures only the current dev's changes.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: QA only checks over-reporting, not under-reporting; does NOT check the reverse: files in the git diff that are NOT in files_modified
success_criteria_results.criterion: QA raises files_modified_underreport_violation (severity: critical) for any file in git diff --name-only <baseline> that is absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and absent from baseline_dirty_paths
success_criteria_results.verification_method: grep -n 'files_modified_underreport_violation' agents/qa.md; read provenance check block at lines 405-455
success_criteria_results.details: files_modified_underreport_violation found at agents/qa.md:434. Reverse check at lines 425-441 iterates diff_files against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created (line 428), excludes baseline_dirty_paths (line 429), raises critical finding (line 436), explicitly notes observed_preexisting is NOT an exclusion (line 441). Severity is 'critical' and primary_cause is 'dev_implementation'.
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/qa.md:425-441 — complete reverse (under-reporting) check block with correct semantics

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: parallel aggregate missing provenance fields: baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, observed_preexisting
success_criteria_results.criterion: Aggregate dev-report JSON contains top-level baseline_head_sha, top-level baseline_dirty_snapshot (both equality-verified from orchestrator dispatch value; aggregate blocked if any worker differs), and nested dev.observed_preexisting (UNION of per-worker lists); both the construction rule text and the example JSON are updated
success_criteria_results.verification_method: grep -n 'baseline_head_sha\|baseline_dirty_snapshot\|observed_preexisting' commands/dev.md; read aggregate construction rule at lines 700-770
success_criteria_results.details: All three fields present: baseline_head_sha at lines 706, 735, 755; baseline_dirty_snapshot at lines 707, 736, 756; dev.observed_preexisting at lines 708, 743, 766. Summary rule (line 706-708): all three fields included. Detailed construction rule (lines 735-737): both baseline_head_sha AND baseline_dirty_snapshot described as equality-verified with explicit block-on-mismatch semantics. dev.status rule (line 737): 'completed iff ALL workers completed AND no baseline_head_sha / baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch'. Example aggregate JSON (lines 755-756, 766): both top-level scalar fields and nested observed_preexisting list present. Both mismatch types are blocking per the AC2 anti-half-pass clause.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/dev.md:706-708 (summary), lines 735-737 (detailed rule), lines 755-756 and 766 (example JSON)

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: files_created derivation misses staged new files: git ls-files --others ... staged (git add) are not 'others'
success_criteria_results.criterion: files_created derivation is UNION of (git ls-files --others --exclude-standard) and (git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A), minus baseline_dirty_paths; QA's check mirrors the same combined set
success_criteria_results.details: In agents/dev.md: line 497 contains the staged-file command within the files_created derivation section at line 495. The derivation is UNION of two sources minus baseline_dirty_paths. Note at line 499 explains non-disjoint design. In agents/qa.md: lines 445 and 452 contain the staged-file command in the files_created provenance check (step 6), referencing both git ls-files --others --exclude-standard and git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A as the combined set.
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/dev.md:495-499 (derivation rule); agents/qa.md:445-452 (QA check mirrors the combined set)

qa._success_criteria_rule: All 3 requirements_decomposition items verified. 6 acceptance criteria verified by grep against modified files.

root_cause_verification.rationale: All three root causes are addressed: (D1) reverse check exists in agents/qa.md step 5a with correct union semantics and baseline_dirty_paths exclusion; (D2) aggregate construction rule in commands/dev.md includes all three provenance fields with equality-verification blocking semantics in both summary and detailed rule, plus example JSON; (D3) files_created derivation in agents/dev.md unions --others and --cached --diff-filter=A, and agents/qa.md check mirrors the same combined set.

build_verification.skip_reason: All three modified files are agent instruction markdown files — no executable code, no UI, no deployed service. Changes only affect how human/AI agents read and follow instructions. No build or deploy step applies.

regression_test_results.details: All 3 files declared in dev.files_modified (agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md) appear in git diff --name-only f79fba1c — no over-reporting violation.

regression_test_results.test: Reverse provenance check — parallel session scope
regression_test_results.details: git diff --name-only f79fba1c shows 199 total files. The 196 extra files are committed changes from other parallel concurrent tasks (task IDs 122910, 122947, 123039, 125300-A/B/C/push, 133650, etc.). The working tree at QA time contains only docs/dev/INDEX.md as uncommitted. This is a known parallel-session limitation: the baseline SHA predates all concurrent tasks. A mechanical reverse check would generate 196 false files_modified_underreport_violations. Per provenance check semantics ('working tree vs baseline SHA — changes are uncommitted at QA time'), the intent is to catch uncommitted work. The target 3 files are committed and absent from working-tree diff against HEAD. Recorded as warning, not critical finding.
Markdown files have no compilation step. File content verified by direct read and grep. No broken structure detected.

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: N/A — no deployed service; changes are agent instruction markdown files only

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 findings: (1) BLOCKER: TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels in all 6 active tests are not waivable under the current QA/test-writer contract; dev had the opportunity to fill in stubs and did not. (2) BLOCKER: provenance check is path-restricted — QA must run full git diff against baseline and compare every path against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created. (3) BLOCKER/MAJOR: diff against baseline includes unrelated hunks from other concurrent tasks (location-complaint tokenization change in commands/dev.md from commit 9c7fd8c, Phase 5 trigger change in agents/qa.md). (4) MAJOR: Dev self-waived a Codex BLOCKER for the TEST_INCOMPLETE stubs. (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY: sentinel count wording — 6 actual pytest.fail sentinels vs 12 broader TEST_INCOMPLETE mentions.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 (TEST_INCOMPLETE BLOCKER): APPLIED — 6 critical findings recorded in failures[], verdict FAIL. Dev contract per test-writer.md requires sentinel removal; BA spec 'No new scripts required' applies to fix implementation scripts, not test bodies. Finding 2 (provenance check path-restricted): APPLIED with nuance — ran full git diff --name-only f79fba1c showing 199 files. The 196 extra files are all committed changes from OTHER parallel concurrent tasks (not this dev's work). The working tree at QA time shows only docs/dev/INDEX.md as uncommitted. The 3 target files are committed. This is a parallel-session limitation where baseline SHA predates all concurrent tasks; mechanical reverse check would generate 196 false violations for other agents' work. Recorded as out_of_scope_observation and warning, not critical finding. Finding 3 (out-of-scope hunks): APPLIED as out_of_scope_observation — the location-complaint tokenization change in commands/dev.md was from commit 9c7fd8c (a separate 'commands session updates' bulk commit). QA scope for this task covers only D1/D2/D3 ACs. The D2-specific changes are cleanly identifiable (lines 706-708, 735-737, 743, 755-756, 766). Finding 4 (Dev self-waiver): APPLIED — QA does not accept Dev's self-waiver per the hard Phase 5 rule. Finding 5 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): Corrected — 6 active pytest.fail TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels (not 12; 12 counted broader comment mentions).

critical_issues: 6 active test files (test_AC1 through test_AC6) contain pytest.fail(TEST_INCOMPLETE: ...) sentinels. The test-writer ran before dev (trigger_gate.fired=true). Dev must fill in the test bodies with static grep assertions before this cycle can close.
AC prose content (D1/D2/D3) is CORRECT — do not re-implement the markdown changes.
Only action needed: fill in 6 test bodies with subprocess or pathlib grep assertions matching the hook_check patterns in the manifest.
refined_context.recommended_approach: For each of the 6 stub files in tests/generated/20260524-125300-D/, replace the pytest.fail(TEST_INCOMPLETE: ...) line with a real assertion. Suggested pattern: read the target file (agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, or agents/dev.md), grep for the AC's pattern string, assert the match count > 0. Preserve the AC_UID, AC_TYPE, function name, and docstring verbatim. Example: 'content = Path(project_root / 'agents/qa.md').read_text(); assert 'files_modified_underreport_violation' in content'.
refined_context.additional_context: The AC prose content in all three files is verified correct and does not need to be changed. The only gap is the unfilled test stubs. The parallel-session provenance check issue (196 extra diff files from concurrent tasks) is a known limitation and does not require a fix — it is logged as an out_of_scope_observation.
